questions.question: Explain the difference between shared memory and message passing IPC models.
expectedPoints: Shared memory allows direct access to common memory region
Message passing involves explicit sending/receiving of messages through kernel
Shared memory is faster but requires explicit synchronization
Message passing has built-in synchronization but higher overhead

questions.question: Describe the producer-consumer problem and explain how it can be solved using semaphores.
expectedPoints: Problem definition: producer generates data, consumer uses data, shared bounded buffer
Need for synchronization: prevent overflow/underflow, ensure mutual exclusion
Semaphore solution: use empty, full, and mutex semaphores
Producer algorithm: wait(empty), wait(mutex), add item, signal(mutex), signal(full)
Consumer algorithm: wait(full), wait(mutex), remove item, signal(mutex), signal(empty)
Implementation considerations: buffer management, circular buffer approach
